title: Metformin and Cancer Risk: A Systematic Review
abstract_text: BACKGROUND: Metformin has been shown to reduce cancer risk. METHODS: We conducted a systematic review. RESULTS: Metformin use was associated with a significant decrease in cancer incidence. CONCLUSIONS: Metformin shows promise.

title: Mechanisms of Metformin in Breast Cancer Prevention
abstract_text: Metformin inhibits breast cancer cell growth through AMPK activation and mTOR pathway inhibition in vitro and in vivo.

title: Metformin Use and Prostate Cancer Survival
abstract_text: We investigated the association between metformin use and survival in patients with prostate cancer. In this cohort study, metformin was associated with improved overall survival.

title: Efficacy of Metformin in Colorectal Cancer
abstract_text: Colorectal cancer cells are sensitive to metformin-induced metabolic stress, leading to apoptosis and cell cycle arrest.

title: Metformin and Pancreatic Cancer: A Meta-Analysis
abstract_text: This meta-analysis of observational studies evaluates the effect of metformin on pancreatic cancer risk, demonstrating a 20% reduction.

title: Low-Dose Metformin and Lung Cancer Progression
abstract_text: Low-dose metformin inhibits progression of lung cancer by targeting the inflammatory microenvironment in mice models.

title: Metformin and Ovarian Cancer Chemosensitivity
abstract_text: Metformin synergizes with cisplatin to reverse chemoresistance in ovarian cancer cells via downregulation of RAD51.

title: Metformin as an Adjuvant Therapy in Melanoma
abstract_text: Adjuvant metformin treatment improves recurrence-free survival in melanoma patients by activating anti-tumor T-cell responses.

title: Metformin inhibits glioblastoma stem cells
abstract_text: Glioblastoma stem cells are selectively targeted by metformin via mitochondrial complex I inhibition, reducing neurosphere formation.

title: Safety and Tolerability of Metformin in Cancer Patients
abstract_text: A phase I trial showing metformin is well-tolerated at therapeutic doses in combination with standard chemotherapy regimens.
